stories.headline: European heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists say
stories.summary: Study also finds high humidity means people in hundreds of cities are enduring their worst ever heat stressThe heatwave scorching western Europe is the most severe and widespread ever and is only possible due to the climate crisis driven by fossil fuel burning, scientists have said.Almost half of Europe’s 850 largest cities are also enduring their worst ever heat stress, a combination of temperature and humidity, they found. Muggier conditions me

stories.headline: ‘Climate change is running rampant’: Europe’s heatwave ‘virtually impossible’ 50 years ago
stories.summary: “Continued fossil-fuel emissions are directly responsible for the disruption people are experiencing this week,” climate scientists warn.

stories.headline: Lundin Oil Executives Accused of Complicity in War Crimes
stories.summary: For one of the first times since Nuremberg, a court is considering whether executives should go to prison for doing business with a brutal government. Lundin Oil’s former chairman Ian Lundin, and Alexandre Schneiter, who was its head of exploration before serving as chief executive officer, are accused of indirectly encouraging atrocities within their concession by the Sudanese military and paramilitary groups. Bloomberg's Simon Marks reports. (S

stories.headline: Stanley: Oil Market Complacent over Hormuz
stories.summary: There are renewed concerns about safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz after an attack on a cargo ship. Meanwhile, oil was on track for a weekly decline after transits through the Strait accelerated. Matt Stanley, Market Engagement Head at Kpler spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the market reaction to the waterway’s operation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence ministerAll of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said.“All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue read

stories.headline: Oil prices ease after spiking over halt to Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan
stories.summary: Brent crude subsides after attack in waterway prompted jump in prices.

stories.headline: Hormuz tanker traffic climbs to 25% of prewar level

Japan to smooth nuclear plant approvals with earlier counterterrorism reviews
Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
stories.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

stories.headline: Row over alleged theft of donations from India's landmark Ram temple
stories.summary: Questions are being raised over the handling of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.headline: The shrinking arguments for degrowth
stories.summary: Economic growth isn’t everything for everyone, but it turns out it’s pretty close

stories.headline: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile push into US consumer market
stories.summary: Move would test whether group can turn sky-high ambition into a mass-market phone business

stories.headline: Meloni redesigns Italy’s election rules to stay in power
stories.summary: Opposition parties denounce ‘authoritarian’ attempt to tilt next year’s general elections in premier’s favour

stories.headline: AI Cost Reality Check Jolts Tech Stocks as Apple Raises Prices
stories.summary: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple raised prices for its products, stoking concern that rising component costs will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

stories.headline: France’s Lescure Sticks With 5% Deficit After Watchdog Rebuke
stories.summary: Finance Minister Roland Lescure reaffirmed his commitment to lower France’s deficit after the country’s fiscal watchdog rebuked the government’s pending plans.

stories.headline: A Chip Wreck in Markets: Markets Snapshot
stories.summary: Micron's earnings numbers came at a delicate moment for the AI industry. Shares of chipmakers and other technology companies had been hammered in recent days on fears that a spending boom was faltering. Questions remain on whether the tech sector can mitigate the boom-and-bust cycles that have characterized the industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Where to Invest 10 Lakh Rupees Amid a Fragile Recovery
stories.summary:
Indian markets are showing signs of recovery, but volatile oil prices, a delayed monsoon, and uneven stock valuations could eat into those gains.

Against the fragile calm, four financial experts point to investment opportunities. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Tumble 9% on Chip Selloff, Triggers Trading Halt
stories.summary: South Korean stocks were hit by a second trading suspension this week as chipmakers plunged, highlighting the market’s sensitivity to swings in global artificial-intelligence sentiment.

stories.headline: Asian Stocks Slump to a Two-Week Low on Tech Rout | The China Show 6/26/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Lingyi iTech Rises in Hong Kong Debut
stories.summary: Kathy Zeng, Chairwoman of Apple supplier Lingyi iTech, says the company has secured major clients in both the US and China as it expands into the humanoid robotics sector. She speaks with Bloomberg's Minmin Low on "Bloomberg: The China Show". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tech Volatility Spills Into Asia | The Asia Trade 6/26/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: How digital payments are reshaping a fast-growing digital banking market
stories.summary: As digital payments become mainstream in Uzbekistan, banks are expanding into lending, insurance and investment products to meet rising consumer demand.

stories.headline: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

stories.headline: Poland's Tusk calls for 'mutual respect' during row with Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.headline: What Are the Paths Forward for the Housing Bill After Trump Refused to Sign It?
stories.summary: President Trump abruptly scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill. It could still become law, with or without a presidential signature. But Mr. Trump could also try to kill it.

stories.headline: Migrants not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border, Supreme Court rules
stories.summary: The 6-3 decision clears the way for the Trump administration to resume allowing federal agents at the border to turn back asylum seekers before they enter.

stories.headline: US, Cuba and Iran join global rescue effort after Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Latin American and European countries also offer solidarity and help to nation already enduring a humanitarian crisisCountries around the world, including Iran, the US and Cuba, have committed to help with rescue efforts in Venezuela after deadly twin earthquakes hit the country.On the evening of 24 June, Venezuela was rocked by two back-to-back earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.2 and 7.5, which killed at least 164 people, injured close to 1,000

stories.headline: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
stories.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

stories.headline: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
stories.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

stories.headline: Johnson Says He Will Send Housing Bill to Trump
stories.summary: After meeting with the president, the speaker said he would send him a housing bill that Mr. Trump declined to sign this week. There was no word on whether he would sign it.

stories.headline: Military Leaders Sought Flu Vaccination Program Weeks Before Outbreak Hit Base
stories.summary: Officials anticipated a problem soon after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lifted the vaccine requirement in April.

stories.headline: John Stockwell, Who Wrote a Tell-All Book About the C.I.A., Dies at 88
stories.summary: A former covert operative, he published his resignation letter in The Washington Post and went on to write “In Search of Enemies,” a book the agency sought to suppress.

stories.headline: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term
stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on powerZimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”.The upper house of Zimbabwe’s parliament voted on Wednesday 75-4 in favour of the constitutional amendments, which will allow Presid

stories.headline: New evidence casts doubt on RFK Jr testimony before Senate
stories.summary: Kennedy repeatedly said 2019 Samoa trip had ‘nothing to do with vaccines’. An email from his then colleague says they were on a vaccine-related ‘mission’New evidence has emerged that Robert F Kennedy Jr was on a vaccine-related “mission” when he visited Samoa ahead of a deadly measles outbreak in 2019, raising further questions about whether the US health secretary lied to the US Senate when he said the trip had “nothing to do with vaccines”.Reco

stories.headline: UN: Venezuela earthquakes will deepen already severe humanitarian crisis
stories.summary: The UN says it is scaling up its response after twin earthquakes devastated Venezuela.

stories.headline: European trade unions call for World Cup-style ‘cooling breaks’ for workers as temperatures soar
stories.summary: The European Trade Union Confederation is pressing the European Commission to set a maximum working temperature law, warning that extreme heat now poses a predictable and preventable threat to workers across the continent.

stories.headline: Beaten and starved: Shock in India as police rescue men from bonded labour
stories.summary: Bonded labour was outlawed in India in 1975, but it still continues in villages and small towns.

stories.headline: Stocks Rattled by Tech Selloff Again, Oil Retreats: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Global stocks slumped to a two-week low after a slide in Apple Inc.’s shares and a report that OpenAI may delay its initial public offering triggered another selloff in the tech sector.

stories.headline: Stellantis, Nissan in Talks to Buy Assets From Marelli
stories.summary: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.

stories.headline: Oil Resumes Decline Even as Ship Attack Clouds Hormuz Outlook
stories.summary: Oil was on track for a weekly decline after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated, although an attack on a cargo ship has renewed concerns about safe passage through the vital waterway.

stories.headline: UPS is losing ground to FedEx
stories.summary: The two logistics giants have similar strategies—and face a common threat

stories.headline: EU's door 'is open to UK', fomer Brexit negotiator Barnier tells Euronews
stories.summary: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

stories.headline: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.headline: White House seeks additional $88 billion from Congress for Iran war, other priorities
stories.summary: The request mostly includes money for the Pentagon, with $67 billion going toward replenishing the military’s stocks of munitions and the cost of sending so many forces to the Middle East.

stories.headline: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
stories.summary: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election integrity bill. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

stories.headline: The 4 GOP senators who broke ranks and voted to block Trump from resuming Iran war
stories.summary: The president seethed at Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul after the war powers resolution passed, saying: “I will get it done, one way or the other.”

stories.headline: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
stories.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

stories.headline: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.headline: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
stories.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

stories.headline: Strange new EV-makers keep appearing in China
stories.summary: If you can make a robo-vacuum, you can make a car

stories.headline: Silicon Valley has much to learn from the spreadsheet jockeys it despises
stories.summary: Even if financial markets disagree

stories.headline: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

stories.headline: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
stories.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

stories.headline: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
stories.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
stories.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

stories.headline: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.headline: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
stories.summary: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

stories.headline: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.headline: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
stories.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
stories.summary: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.headline: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
stories.summary: The agreement followed concerns that continued clashes would undermine the deal to end the war between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.headline: Meet Merlin the Duck: Mexico's World Cup 'ambassador'
stories.summary: The BBC’s Will Grant was amongst the crowds swarming to meet Merlin, a pet duck who went viral for waddling around Mexico City in the Mexican team's jersey.
